title: Force --all when --filter is passed to podman ps
body:
When we filter, it should be out of all containers, not just running ones, by default - this is necessary to ensure Docker compatability.

Fixes #5050

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Should we update the man page to reflect this change?
--filter implies --all
Should we allow for
--all=false --filter ...

```
mheon@bellerophon ~ » sudo docker ps --all=false --filter status=exited
CONTAINER ID        IMAGE               COMMAND             CREATED             STATUS                      PORTS               NAMES
78dcd41c731a        fedora              "ls /"              13 seconds ago      Exited (0) 12 seconds ago                       test
```

Looks like --all being set is unconditional
So I would say more that --all is ignored if --filter is given, and all containers are checked
